feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Build Apps
feed.subtitle: SAP Build Apps Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: When creating a Work Order (WO) in Svc&AssetMgr, long titles display incorrectly
entry.content:
Description:
When creating a Work Order (portrait mode) in Svc&AssetMgr, long titles display incorrectly:

On tablets/iPads (Android/iOS):

 

On mobile devices (iOS/Android): 

Description:
We're observing inconsistent behavior of the '(Unassigned)' label display in Work Orders across different devices:

Attempted Solutions:
Tried adjusting the layout via MDK (Mobile Development Kit), but the inconsistency persists across platforms.

Question:
What alternative approaches could help standardize the display of long titles for all platforms?

entry.title: SAP BUILD importing .mtar file error
entry.content:
Hello,

We changed our plan from free to standard and we are importing our build apps projects. I am experiencing this error for 3 out of 6 projects that we have. I double checked and the project is not existing. I also tried creating a new project with the same name and I was able to create it and delete it.

I found one solution in google, but I don't have access to this link.

https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-builders-knowledge-base/workaround-for-error-importing-projects-in-sap-build-apps/ta-p/220085

Can anyone know what can we do to upload our projects? 

Thanks.

entry.title: "Date Field with input" control in build apps getting wrapped in a container
entry.content:
We are using this control "Date Field with input" in our Build app.

We have placed this control in a container along with some other fields. Now when i click on this control at runtime, it gets wrapped as shown below

One way i was able to get the entire month without getting anything from the calendar display wrapped is if we have enough controls below the date control, then automatically the main container size gets increased. And as a result it appears correctly as below.

Has anyone already faced this or has any pointers on this? Kindly help.

entry.title: Cant Download Base64 Files in SAP Build Apps on Mobile? It only take 1 min to do, Vote to Fix It!!
entry.content:
I’ve raised a feature request on the SAP Influence portal to enable Base64 file downloads in native mobile apps (Android/iOS) using SAP Build Apps. As of now, this functionality is only supported on web apps, which limits real-world mobile use cases like:

✅ Downloading PDFs, receipts, reports, CSVs on the go
✅ Offline access for field workers
✅ Handling file attachments directly from CAP/HANA Cloud responses
✅ Avoiding complex native plugin workarounds in a no-code/low-code platform

This improvement would allow smoother file handling from SAP Build Apps → CAP → mobile, making the ecosystem much more developer- and business-friendly.

📌 Please take a moment to upvote the request here:
👉 https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/346243

Let’s show SAP how much the community needs this!

💬 Feel free to drop your thoughts, use cases, or support in the comments below!

#SAPBuildApps #SAPBTP #SAPCAP #HANACloud #LowCode #MobileApps #SAPInfluence #FioriMobile #Base64Download #SAPCommunity SAP Build Apps

entry.title: Error while building app in App builder
entry.content:
Getting this error when trying to build my app, can anyone suggest why this is happening?

 

AB version: 3.18.2
COMMAND: 

USER_METERING_ENABLED=true SHOW_SUBSCRIPTION_VERIFICATION_NOTIFICATION=false SUBSCRIPTION_VERIFICATION_NOTIFICATION_DISMISSIBLE=true NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=16000 --no-experimental-global-navigator  npxrnv repackage-android -p android -s standalone_bundle -c standalone --ci --yes --packageManager yarn --offline --templates-path /orchestra-cache/4.12.236/ejected_templates --aapt2-path /app/aapt2/aapt2 

FAILED with ERROR: 

warn: You have integration @sba-runtime/integration-builder defined, but it wasn't found in package.json. ERR: Error: Cannot find module '../renative.json'
Require stack:
- ./node_modules/@sba-runtime/integration-builder/lib/config.js
- ./node_modules/@sba-runtime/integration-builder/lib/tasks/taskExport.js
- ./node_modules/@sba-runtime/integration-builder/lib/index.js
- ./node_modules/@rnv/core/lib/modules/index.js
Error: ENOENT: no such file or directory, open '/app/.appgyver/app/renative.private.json'
 at Object.openSync (node:fs:561:18)
 at Object.readFileSync (node:fs:445:35)
 at handleCryptoApiKeys (/app/current/buildHooks/dist/index.js:29381:56)
 at /app/current/node_modules/@rnv/core/src/buildHooks/index.ts:21:50
 at Object.dynatraceOnServiceExecutionIndicator [as doInvoke] (/opt/dynatrace/oneagent-paas/agent/bin/1.307.61.20250226-155505/any/nodejs/nodejsagent.js:3776:22)
error: ⨯ ○ repackage-android: COMMAND: 
jarsigner -keystore /app/current/keystore/keystore.jks -storepass ********* /tmp/repackager-9dXbZB/app-release.aab ********* 
FAILED with ERROR: 
jarsigner error: java.lang.RuntimeException: keystore load: toDerInputStream rejects tag type 107
Error: Command failed with exit code 1: jarsigner -keystore /app/current/keystore/keystore.jks -storepass ********* /tmp/repackager-9dXbZB/app-release.aab *********
jarsigner error: java.lang.RuntimeException: keystore load: toDerInputStream rejects tag type 107
 at makeError (/app/current/node_modules/execa/lib/error.js:60:11)
 at handlePromise (/app/current/node_modules/execa/index.js:118:26)
 at processTicksAndRejections (node:internal/process/task_queues:105:5)
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ ⨯ SUMMARY | 4/23/2025, 1:40:14 AM | rnv@1.3.0-feat-old-overrides.0
│ $ npx rnv repackage-android -p android -s standalone_bundle -c standalone --ci --yes --packageManager yarn --offline --templates-path /orchestra-cache/4.12.236/ejected_templates --aapt2-path /app/aapt2/aapt2
│ warn: You have integration @sba-runtime/integration-builder defined, but it wasn't found in package.json. ERR: Error: Cannot find module '../renative.json'
Require stack:
- ./node_modules/@sba-runtime/integration-builder/lib/config.js
- ./node_modules/@sba-runtime/integration-builder/lib/tasks/taskExport.js
- ./node_modules/@sba-runtime/integration-builder/lib/index.js
- ./node_modules/@rnv/core/lib/modules/index.js
│ error: ⨯ ○ repackage-android: COMMAND: 
jarsigner -keystore /app/current/keystore/keystore.jks -storepass ********* /tmp/repackager-9dXbZB/app-release.aab ********* 
FAILED with ERROR: 
jarsigner error: java.lang.RuntimeException: keystore load: toDerInputStream rejects tag type 107
Error: Command failed with exit code 1: jarsigner -keystore /app/current/keystore/keystore.jks -storepass ********* /tmp/repackager-9dXbZB/app-release.aab *********
jarsigner error: java.lang.RuntimeException: keystore load: toDerInputStream rejects tag type 107
 at makeError (/app/current/node_modules/execa/lib/error.js:60:11)
 at handlePromise (/app/current/node_modules/execa/index.js:118:26)
 at processTicksAndRejections (node:internal/process/task_queues:105:5)
│ Project location: ./platformBuilds/standalone_android │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

 

#appbuilder #error

entry.title: Unable to Create Mobile Application in SAP Build – "Not Subscribed" Message
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm currently following the tutorial "Create Your First MDK Application"  Create Your First MDK Application | SAP Tutorials on SAP BTP.
When I try to create a new application in SAP Build  (first step of Start Your MDK Application in the Editor Task, I see that the "Mobile" application type is not selectable and shows a message stating "Not Subscribed."

I'm using a trial subaccount, and I have already created an instance of Mobile Services and generated the service key. However, it seems that the Mobile Application type in SAP Build is not available due to the subscription status.

Could anyone clarify:

  • What exact subscription is required to enable the Mobile application type in SAP Build?

  • Is it available in trial accounts, or only in productive environments?

  • Are there any additional configurations or entitlements needed?

Thanks in advance for your help!
Davide Bruno

entry.title: Unable to integrate SAP Build Apps using BTP destination- Getting Connection failed error.
entry.content:
Hi,

I am unable to integrate SAP Build apps with BTP destination. Getting "Connection failed" error.

Thank you for help in advance

entry.title: "IMPORT FAILED" error - Can't import project from Build Apps standard plan
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

We upgraded from Build Apps free plan to Build Apps standard plan following this: https://help.sap.com/docs/build-apps/service-guide/upgrade-to-sap-build-apps-standard-edition?q=import

We had 56 projects in the free plan, that we exported as recommended in order to import them back to Build Apps standard plan once installed.

However, instead of having 0 project in the brand new standard plan like in the tutorial video, we noticed that 19 projects were already listed there. We tried to import a project that was not in the list but we keep having the following error message:

IMPORT FAILED
Project eu10.yjdhdjdj… already exists
Correlation Id: 748h67….

We saw the following note but unless mistaken it seems to suggest no solution: https://me.sap.com/notes/0003521702

Just contacted SAP Support about it but if anyone of you knows how to fix this, that would be really appreciated.

Thanks,

BR,

LM.

entry.title: configuration of the ABAP Cloud in SAP Build abap fullstack
entry.content:
Hi

Follow the guide at the link

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/setting-up-the-configuration-of-the-abap-cloud-in-sap-build/bc-p/14084904#M180924

When creating a project from SAP Build ABAP FullStack, the packages are not loaded.

Do you know what could be missing here?

Thanks!

entry.title: OData $batch Request Limitation in SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
I’m trying to update multiple items in SAP S/4HANA using an OData $batch request from  SAP Build Apps . I generate the correct multipart/mixed payload with boundaries in JavaScript. However, when sending this via the HTTP Destination Request, I get the error:
"Invalid body type for binary request. Please provide a file object."

  • SAP Build Apps only accepts a File object (from a File Picker) for binary bodies—not a programmatically created Blob or string.

  • Other body types (JSON, x-www-form-urlencoded, multipart/form-data) are not compatible with OData $batch, which strictly requires multipart/mixed.

  • This blocks seamless, programmatic OData batch operations—users would have to manually upload a file, which breaks UX.

Question:
Has anyone found a workaround for this? Is there a way to send a multipart/mixed OData $batch request from SAP Build Apps without requiring a File Picker or user-uploaded file? Or is this a known limitation with any planned fix?

@Dan_Wroblewski  
Additional information:
JS createBatchPayload:

const records = inputs.input1; // List of selected items
const newGroup = inputs.input2; // e.g., "101", "102", etc.

const batchBoundary = "batch_test";
const changesetBoundary = "changeset_test";

// Log inputs
console.log(" createBatchPayload.js");
console.log(" Records:", JSON.stringify(records, null, 2));
console.log(" New Purchasing Group:", newGroup);

// Safety check
if (!records || !Array.isArray(records) || records.length === 0) {
console.warn(" No records provided to batch payload.");
return { result: "" };
}

if (!newGroup || typeof newGroup !== "string" || newGroup.trim() === "") {
console.warn(" Missing or invalid Purchasing Group input.");
return { result: "" };
}

let batchParts = [];

records.forEach((item, index) => {
const patchBlock = [
`--${changesetBoundary}`,
"Content-Type: application/http",
"Content-Transfer-Encoding: binary",
`Content-ID: ${index + 1}`,
"",
`PATCH PurchaseReqnItem(PurchaseRequisition='${item.Document}',PurchaseRequisitionItem='${item.DocumentItem}') HTTP/1.1`,
"Content-Type: application/json",
"If-Match: *",
"",
JSON.stringify({ PurchasingGroup: newGroup }),
""
].join(" ");

batchParts.push(patchBlock);
});

const payload = [
`--${batchBoundary}`,
`Content-Type: multipart/mixed; boundary=${changesetBoundary}`,
"",
...batchParts,
`--${changesetBoundary}--`,
`--${batchBoundary}--`
].join(" ");

console.log("✅ Final Batch Payload: ", payload);

return { result: payload };

JS converToBlob:

// Input: outputs.createBatchPayload.result (text payload)
// Output: Blob ready for HTTP request

const payloadText = inputs.input1;
const boundary = "batch_test";

console.log(" convertToBlob.js");
console.log(" Input payload length:", payloadText.length);

// Validate payload structure
if (!payloadText.includes(`--${boundary}`)) {
console.error(" Missing batch boundary markers");
return { blob: null };
}

// Create Blob with correct MIME type
const blob = new Blob([payloadText], {
type: `multipart/mixed; boundary=${boundary}`
});

console.log(" Created Blob:", {
size: blob.size,
type: blob.type
});

// Optional verification
const reader = new FileReader();
reader.onload = () => {
console.log(" Blob content sample:", reader.result.substring(0, 200));
};
reader.readAsText(blob);

return { blob: blob };

entry.title: SAP Build Apps combo box version 1.1.9
entry.content:
Has anyone been facing issues with latest combo box version 1.1.9 in build apps? We have raised a ticket with SAP but no updates from SAP. The latest combo box version is failing to fetch and display data despite being mapped to the backend.

#combobox #buildapps

Thanks,

Sneha

entry.title: Status 502
entry.content:
Hello,

 

I am doing the "Connect your SAP Build App to a Public API" and i have a problem at Step 3.

At step 3, when i have to test the "retrieve" config, it gives me a: "message": "Request failed with status code 502". I don't understand what i did wrong. I followed the tutorial step by step (i even tried again from scratch and gave me the same thing).

Thank you.

entry.title: Android.permission.READ_MEDIA_IMAGES and Android.permission.READ_MEDIA_VIDEO - Error in Play Store
entry.content:
Hi Friends,

I’m using the SAP Build Apps Free Edition and recently encountered an issue while trying to publish an update for my app on the Google Play Store.

The Play Store is flagging the following permissions and requesting a justification for their use:

  • android.permission.READ_MEDIA_IMAGES

  • android.permission.READ_MEDIA_VIDEO

However, my app doesn’t access any media files on the device. All content (text and images) is built into the app. It only accesses a couple of external websites and a location—features that rely on device tools like the web browser and Google Maps.

Could you please guide me on how to resolve this permission issue so I can successfully publish the update?

Also, is there a way to remove or modify these permissions before generating the final build in the Free Edition? Else, how do I write an explanation/justification to Google Play Store for approving the above permissions and going ahead with publishing my updates?

Thank you in advance for your support!

Best regards,
Paul

entry.title: SAP Build Library and SAP Build Apps Integration Issue
entry.content:
Hi guys,
I have one demo process in my SAP Build Process Automation Application and I have deployed and published it.


Now, in my SAP Build Apps, I have created an application and in Integration tab, I selected SAP Build Library, I can see my process there, but when I click on it shows Page not found.


I have also configured sap_process_automation_service_user_access destination in my BTP for the instance of Build Process automation and test shows successful & 401. Also, added that destination in control tower's destination in build lobby. Please help.

In the attached image we can see the published process available in cloud library.

SAP Build Library

Page Not Found Error for process

Thanks & Regards,
Marsh

entry.title: I am currently working on a project involving SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content: I need to integrate it into an existing BAPI in SAP S/4HANA. However, I'm facing an issue: I don't have access to the BAPI through the Actions group in SAP Build Process Automation. Therefore, I'm looking for a solution to connect to this BAPI via SAP BTP.

entry.title: SAP Build App event handler issue
entry.content:
Hey guys

I have configured sap_process_automation_service_user_access destination in my BTP for the instance of Build Process automation and test shows successful & 401. Also, added that destination in control tower's destination in build lobby

I have also created a Web & Mobile application in Sap Build App 

And I have integrated a process (Sap Build Automation) into Sap Build App. 

When I preview my application, I'm encountering an error in SAP Build App: Unknown node id: page:1:component:<ID>:onFocus/onBlur/onKeyPress/onChange when interacting with input fields. The error mentions Abort fetching component for route: "/[appId]/[appName]/[...params]". I don't have any logic configured for these events, can anyone help me identify the cause of this issue?

Thank in advance, 

Giang

entry.title: Build Apps Issue
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are working on a scenario where in we are using build apps. However, we are facing couple of challenges.

1. During the loading of home page, We want a list item to be shown in the initial screen. We have created a data variable which gives a array but it does not show up in the list item page. However, using toggle, we can see the value being returned. Attached screen shot:

Build App classical entity rest API destination integration which is used for data variables.

Same is called inside flow logic ( getdefectdata 😞

However, when using list items repeated, it does not show up line item data. However, while using toast functionality, it shows the data up. 

2. We want users to capture image from camera and use the same image for uploading to Hana Cloud for which we were using filepath location and filetobase64 converter. However, it seems once the converter gets called, system gets stuck up. We dont want users to take picture separately and then come to apps for better user experience. How can this be fixed ? I have created a parallal branch just to show up. Ideally its only 1 flow that gets executed and in that flow it gets stuck up completely and app needs to be stopped. I am using the Build App testing platform in android to test this. We have created a BLOB storage in Hanacloud and is trying to update that data

Can you please suggest on this. Is this something that we are missing or it looks like a product issue and we need to reach out to SAP ?

Thanks

Tirthankar

entry.title: Is it possible in Visual Cloud Functions to define relations between entities?
entry.content:
Hi experts,
ist it possible to define relations between entities in Visual Cloud Functions?
E.g. I have entity "Sales Order Header" with Customer, OrderDate, etc. and then I have "Sales Order Items" with Product, Quantity, Price, etc.
and I would like to make a reference, saying, this sales order item belongs to this sales order.

Any help would be appreciated.

Best,

Simon

entry.title: Using Visual Cloud Functions - error message regarding CSRF Protection
entry.content:
Dear experts,

I'm trying to use Visual Cloud Functions and why I try to add some data directly in the backend, I get the error message (see also picture1): 

Request failed with status code 403: Forbidden
If this destination has CSRF protection, the BuildApps.RequiresCsrf = true flag needs to be added to it.

I have also tried to store a data entry via SAP Build App, but there I get the same error.
When I try to set configure the CSRF functionality in BackendConfiguration of the SAP Build App, I am informed that I need to configure BTP first. (picture2).
 
It would be great to get some hints how to resolve this.
 
Best,
Simon

entry.title: How to bring the Approve/Reject step from SAP My Inbox into my custom SAP Build App
entry.content:
Hey guys, 

I'm building an application, allow admin can approve/reject those order when user create their order in sap build process automation

So the question i want to know is : How to bring the Approve/Reject step from SAP My Inbox into my custom SAP Build Apps application

Thank in advance, 

Giang
